name: Ingushetia
description: Hearty, distinctive highland cuisine — chapilgash, dulkh-khaltam and meat dishes with spicy sauces. Simple ingredients prepared by recipes passed down for centuries

name: Chechnya
description: Zhizhig-galnash, chepalgash and rich broth are the heart of the Chechen table. The cuisine is filling and unadorned, yet every dish is about real taste

name: Dagestan
description: The most multi-ethnic region of the Caucasus — and the cuisine matches it. Khinkal, chudu, kurze — each people of Dagestan added something of its own

name: Kabardino-Balkaria
description: Lyagur, khychyns with cheese and potato, ayran — the cuisine of the Balkars and Kabardians looks simple but is rich in flavour

name: Karachay-Cherkessia
description: Khychyns here are the main dish and a reason to gather the whole family. Plus ayran, lamb and pastries made from mountain flour

name: North Ossetia
description: Ossetian pies with various fillings are not just food but part of the culture. Three pies on the table are a sign of respect and good wishes
